channel.title: Java on My blog
channel.description: Recent content in Java on My blog

item.title: Java Exception
item.description: Exception mechanism helps developers find issues of their programs. Its base class is java.lang.Throwable which consists of java.lang.Error and java.lang.Exception.

item.title: Java Map
item.description: In this post, I will introduce HashMap, LinkedHashMap and Hashtable in Java as well as their corresponding methods.

item.title: Java Set
item.description: In this post, I will introduce HashSet and LinkedHashSet in Java as well as their corresponding methods.

item.title: Java Generic Programming
item.description: In this post, I will introduce generic programming in Java and talk about how to define and use generic class, generic method, generic interface and generic wildcard.

item.title: Java Iterator
item.description: In this post, I will talk about Java iterator which is a powerful tool for us to traverse elements. In addition, enhanced for loop will also be introduced which can simplify the code for element traversal.

item.title: Java Collection
item.description: In this post, I will talk about Java collection. Some well-known containers such as ArrayList, TreeSet are implementing classes of their respective interfaces which inherit from the collection interface. Therefore, it is a good idea to learn methods from the collection interface because all of its derived implementing classes will acquire the same methods.

item.title: Java Predefined Class
item.description: In this post, I will talk about some predefined classes as well as commonly used APIs in Java. The post will cover object class, date class, date format class, calendar class, system class, string builder class and wrapper class. In addition, I will demonstrate some methods of those classes and show some examples.

item.title: Java Inner Class
item.description: If an object contains another object, then this means that a class includes another class. For example, the relationship between cars and engines. In this case, the engine class is called an inner class. In this post, I will explore the properties of three types of inner classes (Member Inner Class / Local Inner Class / Anonymous Inner Class).

item.title: 'Final' keyword in Java
item.description: ‘Final’ keyword represents ‘ultimate’ and ‘unchangeable’. In this post, I will talk about four usages of the ‘final’ keyword, including modifying a class, a method, local variables (basic and reference types) and a member variable.

item.title: Java Polymorphism
item.description: In this post, I will talk about the concepts and benefits of polymorphism. Then, I will point out some important properties about using class variables and methods when polymorphism happens. Moving on, object down casting and ‘instanceof’ method will be briefly introduced. Finally, I will demonstrate a case study combining the knowledge of using interface and polymorphism.

item.title: Java Interface
item.description: An interface is a reference type in Java. It is similar to class. It is a collection of abstract methods. A class implements an interface, thereby inheriting the abstract methods of the interface. Along with abstract methods, an interface may also contain constants, default methods, static methods, and nested types (TutorialsPoint 2022).
